name: Accessibility, SXO, Checkout, And Customer Journey Smoke

scope.safetyBoundary: No booking, checkout payment, order submission, contact form submission, login, account action, provider write, or private data action was submitted.

positiveEvidence.evidence: Rendered homepage has skip link, main/nav/footer landmarks, crawlable core links, focusable controls, and visible address, phone, email, booking, and catalog paths.

Rendered DPF page confirms service, location, pricing/quote context, process/duration context, and no internal review text. Booking modal opens with focus in the license-plate field, dialog role, visible label, cancel/close controls, and disabled next step until selections are made.
Rendered product page exposes exact brand/model, variant dimensions, price, availability, seller, delivery/terms context, Product JSON-LD, and add-to-cart action.

Add-to-cart opens a cart state with product and price. Checkout action navigates to /checkout, removes canonical, sets robots noindex,nofollow, preserves product and price, shows terms/payment context, and keeps the submit button disabled before required data.
Contact page renders indexable contact content with address, phone, email, hours, and actionable tel/mailto/maps links.
npm run checkout:check and npm run commerce:check passed.

findings.title: Global header exposes a visible unnamed button.
findings.evidence: Homepage, DPF service, product, catalog, checkout, and contact route audits found a visible button with no accessible name in the top navigation area.
findings.impact: Screen-reader users and browser agents cannot determine the control purpose. This is a shared accessibility and agent-operability defect.
findings.verification: Rendered DOM/accessibility-tree audit shows every visible button has a stable accessible name.

findings.title: Checkout contains unlabeled checkbox inputs and ambiguous toggle names.
findings.evidence: Checkout start rendered two visible checkbox inputs with no label or aria-label; checkout buttons also exposed ambiguous names such as 'on'.
findings.impact: High-impact purchase flow is not fully accessible or agent-operable because users cannot reliably understand agreement/selection controls before order confirmation.
findings.verification: Checkout form audit shows all inputs/toggles have visible and programmatic labels, required state, and clear selected state.

findings.title: Catalog search and product-list actions are not sufficiently labelled.
findings.evidence: Catalog license plate input has placeholder 'ABC-123' but no visible/programmatic label. Repeated product-list buttons are generically named 'Lisää' without product-specific accessible names.
findings.impact: Vehicle lookup and product add actions are ambiguous for assistive technology and browser agents.
findings.verification: Catalog audit shows plate input label, useful autocomplete/format hints, and product-specific Add buttons such as 'Add [product name] to cart'.

findings.title: Product media/gallery controls include unnamed buttons.
findings.evidence: Product detail page exposed two visible unnamed product media buttons near the product image area.
findings.impact: Product media exploration is harder for keyboard/screen-reader users and browser agents.
findings.verification: All product gallery controls have accessible names such as open image, previous image, next image, or select thumbnail.

findings.title: Rendered route console shows repeated unused preload warning.
findings.evidence: Browser console reported repeated warnings for a preloaded /_json/.../_index.json resource not used shortly after load.
findings.impact: Performance hygiene issue; no JavaScript errors were observed in E-3 smoke.
findings.verification: Console smoke has zero errors and no recurring preload warnings after route changes.

findings.title: Mobile viewport customer journey evidence is unavailable in E-3.
findings.evidence: Available browser MCP tools in this run did not expose viewport resize/device emulation. Desktop rendered viewport was 1517x935.
findings.impact: Release cannot claim mobile journey parity from this E-3 run.
findings.verification: Run mobile viewport/device smoke for homepage, service booking, catalog, product/cart, checkout, and contact.

commands.command: mcp__playwright.browser_navigate/evaluate rendered smoke for homepage, DPF service, booking modal, catalog, product, cart, checkout, and contact

remainingReleaseBlockersFromPriorTasks: E-1 Figma Make source still needs four service placeholder patches and preview smoke.
next: E-4 - Drift Baseline, Monitoring, Incident Runbook, And Owners
releaseDecision: E-3 can close as rendered customer-journey evidence. It does not make the site release-ready because E-2 production blockers remain and E-3 accessibility/mobile findings require remediation or owner-approved exception.
